Medi Assist: Partners LawtonAsia to Expand AI Health-Tech in Southeast Asia

• 🌏 Global Expansion: Medi Assist enters Southeast Asia via strategic tie-up with Thailand-based LawtonAsia. 

• 🤖 AI Platform Deployment: LawtonAsia to adopt Medi Assist’s AI-driven health & travel insurance platform to improve claims experience. 

• ⚡ Efficiency Gains Expected: Solution aims to reduce turnaround time and enhance transparency for policyholders and corporates. 

• 🚀 Implementation Underway: Testing has started in Thailand with full go-live planned in coming weeks. 

🟢 Impact: Positive
Strategic international expansion and AI platform adoption strengthen Medi Assist’s growth visibility and global positioning.

Krishanveer Forge : Co. Grants Right of Way to Hydrolines

• 🛣️ Agreement Executed: Company signed Right of Way agreement with Hydrolines (Bangalore) Pvt Ltd for land access.

• 📍 Land Details: Easement granted on ~2,402 sq. meters at Kharabwadi, Chakan, Pune.

Administrative land access arrangement with no immediate financial inflow; limited near-term earnings impact.

SJS Enterprises : Co. Signs Wind Power Deal

• ⚡ Renewable Tie-Up: SJS Enterprises entered agreements with Doddanavar Global Energy and DB Renews for annual supply of up to 36 lakh units of wind power. 

• 💰 Strategic Investment: Company subscribed to 28,800 shares of DB Renews for ₹72 lakh, gaining 2.08% stake. 

• 🏭 Captive Power Benefit: Post acquisition, SJS will become a captive consumer of wind power, supporting energy cost optimisation. 

• 🌱 Green Energy Push: DB Renews operates a 27 MW wind project in Karnataka, strengthening SJS’s renewable footprint. 

🟢 Impact: Positive
Move improves energy sustainability and may help reduce long-term power costs, though financial impact is modest in near term.

Aequs Invests in Defence JV, To Hold 33.33% Stake

• 🚀 Strategic Investment: Aequs signs Letter of Subscription to invest in Ajna Aerospace & Defence Pvt Ltd JV. 

• 🛩️ Focus Area: Target company operates in UAV/UAS and autonomous aerial platforms for defence and civilian use. 

• 💰 Investment Size: Company to invest about ₹10.01 crore via equity and CCPS subscription. 

• 📊 Stake Post Deal: Aequs will hold 33.33% stake (fully diluted) in the JV. 

• 📅 Timeline: Transaction expected to be completed by March 31, 2026. 

🟢 Impact: Positive
Entry into high-growth defence UAV space supports Aequs’ long-term diversification and strategic growth outlook.

reports.text:
Sky Gold & Diamonds: Shifts to Asset-Light Expansion Under Sky Gold 3.0

• 🔄 Strategy Pivot: Company to monetise earlier acquired land (~₹105 Cr) and move to leased manufacturing model. 

• ⚡ Capital Efficiency Focus: Asset-light approach aimed at faster execution, stronger cash flows and better capital deployment. 

• 📉 Debt Reduction Plan: Proceeds from land sale and improved cash flows expected to cut borrowings by 20%+ in FY27. 

• 🏭 Expansion Intact: Management says capacity expansion timelines remain unchanged despite strategy shift. 

• 🛡️ Promoter Backstop: Promoter family to buy the land if sale not completed within six months, protecting the company. 

🟢 Impact: Positive
Asset-light shift improves capital efficiency, supports deleveraging, and strengthens long-term return ratios.

reports.text:
**Small and MIDs Performance In RBI Rate Cut Cycles**

**During Rate Cuts**
• Returns were mixed
• Midcap & Smallcap delivered negative returns in some cycles
• Volatility remained high

**After Rate Cut Cycle Ends**
• SMIDs outperformed meaningfully
• Strong recovery phase observed
• Liquidity and growth improved

**Historical Trend (25 Years)**
• Every rate cut cycle above **100 bps** followed by sharp rebound
• Consistent recovery across macro environments
• Strong **2-year absolute returns** post easing

**Why It Happens**
• Lower rates boost liquidity
• Improves earnings growth visibility
• Drives valuation re-rating
• Works better when starting valuations are reasonable

**Investor Insight**
• Bigger opportunity often **after easing phase**
• Patience during cuts, positioning for recovery
• SMIDs benefit most in liquidity-driven upcycles

Pan India Corporation Ltd: Signs Tripartite Agreement With New RTA

• 🔄 RTA Transition Completed: Company entered a tripartite agreement on Feb 26, 2026 with Nivis Corpserve LLP (new RTA) and Abhipra Capital Ltd (old RTA). 

• 📁 Data & Records Handed Over: All physical and electronic shareholder records have been transferred to the new registrar. 

• ✅ Regulatory Compliance: Move executed under SEBI (LODR) Regulation 7(4) requirements. 

• 🤝 Smooth Transition Assurance: Old RTA to cooperate and new RTA to process investor requests based on transferred data. 

🟡 Impact: Neutral
Administrative change in share transfer agent; no direct impact on business fundamentals or financials.

Zydus Lifesciences: Pembrolizumab Biosimilar Clears Key Clinical Milestone

• 🧪 Clinical Success: FYB206 pivotal PK study met primary endpoint, demonstrating bioequivalence with Keytruda®. 

• 🇺🇸 Market Entry Prep: Company preparing regulatory dossiers for near-term USFDA Biologics License Application (BLA) filing. 

• 🌎 Strategic Licensing: Zydus holds exclusive rights for the U.S. and Canada via in-licensing from Formycon AG. 

• 💊 Large Market Opportunity: Reference drug Keytruda generated about $31.6 billion global sales in 2025, indicating strong biosimilar potential. 

🟢 Impact: Positive
Successful clinical milestone strengthens Zydus’ oncology biosimilar pipeline and positions it for entry into the high-value North American immuno-oncology market.

Banks May Raise CD & Bulk Deposit Rates Amid Liquidity Squeeze

Funding Cost Pressure
• Banks likely to raise CD and bulk deposit rates in March
• Driven by year-end credit demand
• Liquidity conditions tightening

CD Rate Trend
• 3-month CD rates crossed 7% (~7.10%)
• May rise toward 7.50% if loan demand sustains
• Short-term borrowing becoming costlier

Credit-Deposit Stress
• Credit-deposit ratio at ~82% (record high)
• Reflects elevated funding pressure
• Loan growth outpacing deposit mobilisation

Liquidity Conditions
• System liquidity around ₹2.50 lakh crore in February
• March-end balance sheet adjustments add strain
• GST outflows impacting liquidity

Regulatory & Structural Factors
• Lower Liquidity Coverage Ratio (LCR) buffer
• Elevated loan-to-deposit ratios
• Greater dependence on short-term instruments

Market Influence
• Debt mutual fund flows critical for liquidity
• January inflows improved after December outflows
• Volatility in flows can impact rates

Investor Perspective
• Funding cost pressure may compress NIMs near term
• Deposit competition likely to intensify
• CASA mobilisation becomes critical
• Temporary spike possible till March-end

Short-term liquidity tightness may persist, keeping funding costs elevated into fiscal year-end.

Advanced Packaging Seen As India’s Entry Into Global Chip Supply Chains

Strategic Opportunity
• Advanced packaging identified as key integration point
• Moves India beyond basic assembly and testing
• Aligns with global semiconductor value chain shifts

Technology Transition
• Shift from pure lithography scaling
• Rising focus on materials engineering
• Growth in 3D chips and heterogeneous integration
• Advanced packaging gaining strategic importance

India Advantage
• Strong semiconductor design talent pool
• Large engineering workforce
• Faster execution critical to capture opportunity

Applied Materials Expansion
• Scaling 300 mm wafer India Validation Center
• New facility inaugurated in Bengaluru
• Committed $400 million over four years for collaborative engineering centre

Global Demand Context
• AI-led semiconductor demand remains strong
• Over 100 new fabs being tracked globally
• Structural multi-year growth cycle intact

Challenges
• Power availability constraints
• Infrastructure readiness gaps
• Need for ecosystem-level coordination

Investor Perspective
• Advanced packaging offers lower capex entry vs full-scale fabs
• Opportunity to build niche, high-value positioning
• Beneficiary segments: materials, equipment, testing solutions
• Long-term strategic play in global semiconductor supply chain

Advanced packaging can serve as India’s pragmatic and scalable gateway into next-generation semiconductor manufacturing.

RE Ministry Plans Ancillary Market Push For Grid Stability

Policy Initiative
• MNRE to initiate discussions on ancillary market framework
• Issue taken up with Central Electricity Authority
• Aim to address frequency and voltage fluctuations

Key Focus Areas
• Frequency control services
• Voltage support mechanisms
• Black start capability
• Grid-forming inverters deployment
• Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) integration

Trigger For Action
• Rapid renewable capacity additions
• Rising grid integration challenges
• Need for structured system reserves

Market Development
• CEA preparing paper on ancillary service opportunities
• Potential new revenue stream for service providers
• Formal market-based compensation model likely

Infrastructure Alignment
• Transmission expansion being prioritised
• Grid strengthening alongside renewable rollout
• Focus on long-term system reliability

Structural Context
• India now 3rd largest solar market globally
• Renewables forming rising share of installed capacity
• Grid modernisation becoming critical

Investor Perspective
• Positive for BESS and grid technology players
• Opens ancillary revenue pool for power companies
• Improves long-term renewable integration stability
• Supports sustainable capacity expansion

Structured ancillary market development is key to balancing India’s accelerating renewable growth trajectory.

US Solar Duty Pushes SEZ Units Toward Domestic Market

US Trade Action
• US imposed 125.87% countervailing duty on Indian solar imports
• Preliminary decision; final ruling by July 6, 2026
• May change if bilateral trade deal materialises

Export Exposure
• India exported $1.2 billion solar panels to US in 2025
• Down from $1.5 billion in 2024
• US accounts for 98% of India’s solar exports
• High concentration risk highlighted

Immediate Impact
• US importers must post duty deposits
• Landed cost increases significantly
• Risk of margin pressure and order cancellations
• Contract disputes possible for committed shipments

Industry Response
• Industry bodies seek permission for SEZ units to sell domestically
• Aim to offset export disruption
• Push to diversify revenue streams

Strategic Implication
• Reduces over-dependence on single geography
• Strengthens case for domestic solar demand absorption
• Aligns with India’s renewable capacity expansion plans

Legal Angle
• WTO challenge possible
• Litigation process likely prolonged
• Near-term uncertainty persists

Investor Perspective
• Near-term earnings risk for export-focused players
• Domestic market shift could cushion revenue impact
• Policy support critical for SEZ flexibility
• Sector volatility expected until final US determination

Trade action increases concentration risk but may accelerate domestic market integration for Indian solar manufacturers.

DGCA Introduces 48-Hour Free Cancellation Window

New Passenger-Friendly Rule
• 48-hour free cancellation and modification window introduced
• Applicable if flight is 7 days away (domestic)
• Applicable if flight is 15 days away (international)
• Effective from March 26

Refund Framework
• Airlines must process refunds within 14 days
• Credit card refunds within 7 working days
• Applies to bookings via airlines or travel agents

Cancellation Charge Cap
• Fees cannot exceed basic fare + fuel surcharge
• No excessive penalty charges allowed
• Clear disclosure of refund break-up mandatory

Credit Shell Option
• Passengers may opt for credit shell vouchers
• Provides flexibility instead of cash refund
• Transferable in medical emergency cases

Medical Emergency Clause
• Refund or transferable credit shell permitted
• Enhances passenger protection norms

• Improve transparency in airline pricing
• Reduce passenger complaints
• Standardise refund timelines across carriers

• May impact ancillary fee income for airlines

Govt Mandates Nationwide Sale Of E20 Petrol From April

Regulatory Directive
• Sale of petrol blended with up to 20% ethanol (E20) mandated from April 1
• Minimum RON 95 specification required
• Applicable across all states and Union Territories
• Directive issued by Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas

Technical Aspect
• RON measures resistance to engine knocking
• Higher RON ensures better performance and efficiency
• Most new vehicles compatible with E20 blending
• Blending beyond 20% requires engineering changes

Strategic Objective
• Reduce crude oil import dependence
• Strengthen India’s energy security
• Boost domestic ethanol production capacity

Industry Impact
• Provides long-term demand visibility for ethanol producers
• Positive for sugar mills and grain-based distilleries
• Supports farmers growing sugarcane and maize
• Enhances rural income ecosystem

Economic Implication
• Ethanol blending helping reduce oil import bill
• Structural shift toward biofuel integration
• Strengthens domestic energy value chain

Investor Perspective
• Positive for ethanol manufacturers and integrated sugar players
• Improves revenue stability through policy-backed demand
• Long-term structural tailwind for agri-linked energy businesses

Policy move reinforces India’s biofuel roadmap with nationwide implementation clarity.

Govt Plans Value-Addition Linked PLI For Smartphones

Policy Shift In PLI Structure
• Next phase of smartphone PLI may link incentives to domestic value addition
• Current scheme based on incremental sales ends March 31
• Proposed model ties payouts to predefined localisation thresholds

Current Scenario
• Exports, value addition, employment monitored but not mandatory
• Domestic value addition improved to 35–40% by FY26-end
• Initially started at 15–20%
• Still below earlier 40% expectation

Strategic Objective
• Move beyond basic assembly operations
• Push deeper localisation of components
• Encourage manufacturing of production equipment locally

Ecosystem Development
• Separate electronics components manufacturing scheme launched
• Focus on strengthening domestic supply chain
• Reduce import dependency over time

Structural Transition
• Shift from volume-driven incentives
• Move toward “beyond assembly” strategy
• Aim to build complete electronics ecosystem in India

Investor Takeaway
• Favors players with strong localisation capabilities
• Likely to benefit component manufacturers
• Margin profile may improve with deeper value chain integration
• Long-term positive for India’s electronics manufacturing story

Policy direction clearly signals quality and depth over pure output growth.

Proposed Rollout Timeline

• Expected launch around Mid-2026
• Early-stage ecosystem integration

Payment Infrastructure Strategy
• Likely support for UPI and card-based payments
• Alignment with India’s digital payments backbone
• Engagement with Mastercard and Visa

Market Opportunity
• India has 750+ million smartphone users
• Among fastest-growing digital payments markets
• Strong structural shift toward cashless economy

Competitive Landscape
• Dominated by Google Pay, PhonePe, Amazon Pay, and Paytm
• Apple likely to focus on premium segment first
• Ecosystem-driven adoption strategy

Technology Differentiation
• Face ID / Touch ID based authentication
• Tap-to-pay and seamless online transactions
• High security and privacy positioning

Strategic India Focus
• Part of broader India expansion strategy
• Expanding local manufacturing and retail footprint
• India emerging as key iPhone production hub
• Supply chain diversification beyond China

Investor Perspective
• Strengthens Apple services revenue play
• Enhances ecosystem stickiness in India
• Long-term structural growth opportunity
• Competitive but high-growth market

India remains central to Apple’s next phase of global expansion.

NSE Chief Pushes F&O Eligibility Norms

Proposal Overview  
- NSE MD Ashish Chauhan calls for minimum qualifying criteria for F&O trading  
- Aims to protect lower-income investors from speculative losses  

Global Benchmark  
- US model: $25,000 minimum margin balance required  
- India currently: No eligibility threshold  

Rationale  
- Curb excessive retail speculation  
- Align with Singapore's stricter access norms  

Regulatory Context  
- Follows Budget STT hikes: Equity futures to 0.05% (from 0.02%), options premium to 0.15% (from 0.1%)  
- Exchanges/regulators tightening derivatives access  

Strategic Intent  
- Enhanced risk management focus  
- Promotes derivatives market stability  

Key Takeaway:  
NSE's eligibility push signals multi-pronged retail protection strategy — F&O participation set for quality-over-quantity shift.

- Transit-oriented development

India C&I Renewable Energy Capacity To Reach 57 GW By FY28

Capacity Expansion Outlook

• C&I renewable capacity projected at 57 GW by FY28
• Expected to reach ~40 GW by FY26
• Implies strong multi-year growth visibility

Growth Drivers

• Corporate decarbonisation and net-zero commitments
• Tariff arbitrage between open-access RE and grid tariffs
• Long-term PPAs improving revenue visibility
• Renewable Purchase Obligations (RPOs) compliance push
• Attractive IRR for developers

Policy & Regulatory Support

• Green Energy Open Access (GEOA) Rules, 2022 enabling direct sourcing
• Use of existing transmission infrastructure
• Industrial states offering fast-track approvals and incentives
• Policy clarity reducing execution risk

Demand Strength

• C&I remains India’s largest power-consuming segment
• Rising ESG focus from corporates
• Stable and strong counterparty credit profiles

Investor Takeaway

• Visible pipeline till FY28
• Long-term contracted cash flows via PPAs
• Strong policy tailwinds
• Structural shift toward clean energy adoption

Medium-term opportunity remains robust for developers focused on C&I renewable portfolio expansion.

SEBI Revamps MF Rules: Gold/Silver Exposure Upto 35%

Expanded Asset Flexibility  
- Equity funds can allocate upto 35% to gold, silver, InvITs  
- Moves beyond traditional equity/debt instruments  

Solution Schemes Frozen  
- No fresh inflows into retirement/children's funds (₹60,000 cr AUM)  
- Schemes to merge with similar allocation profiles  

Stricter Categorisation  
- Multi-cap funds: Minimum 25% each in large/mid/small-cap  
- Sectoral/thematic overlap capped at 50% with other equity schemes  
- Overlap calculated by common securities + weightage  

Investor Protection Focus  
- Ensures "true to label" scheme positioning  
- Curbs mis-selling and scheme duplication  

Strategic Timing  
- Boosts commodity diversification amid global gold/silver rally  

Key Takeaway:  
SEBI's reforms enhance flexibility while enforcing discipline — gold/silver access positions equity funds for uncertainty.

- ₹110 cr invested (Cyclops ₹80 cr, Nostradamus ₹30 cr)  
- Target: Slash underwriting from 40 to 10 hours in 6 months  
- Needs robust digital infrastructure buildout  

Key Takeaway:  
Collections buffer regulatory risks while AI investments drive underwriting efficiency and scalable growth.

*CLSA on Tata Motors CV*

Expect TMCV to generate robust FCF over next two years, helping the balance sheet become lean again by FY28CL - even after taking debt for the €3.8bn Iveco acquisition.
After three muted years, Iveco volumes should accelerate on a lower base ahead of the Euro 7 shift in FY28-29. 
In addition, as EV adoption in LCVs takes off across India & EU, TMCV-Iveco combination brings meaningful scale and supply-chain synergies, giving a strategic benefit to the merged entity.

*Citi on MFI – Bihar Bill*
Bill explicitly exempts the lending operations of banks/NBFCs
See no direct repercussions for listed entities
Multiple lending or adverse shifts in repayment behavior stemming from cultural influences could have indirect ramifications for banks/NBFCs

*MS on MFI – Bihar Bill*
Bihar passes bill to regulate MFI activities; mandates lender registration with state Finance Department
Special courts to address recovery-related grievances, aimed at curbing unethical practices
Bihar accounts for ~15% of the total MFI loan book, making it the largest state exposure
Move may weigh on investor sentiment, though actual financial impact could remain limited
Asset quality expected to improve, but loan growth unlikely to revert to earlier peak levels
Lenders likely to maintain or reduce microfinance exposure amid earnings and valuation volatility

*Motilal Oswal on CGD*
Valuations across the CGD space have corrected meaningfully, reflecting concerns around input gas cost volatility, uncertainty over APM/New Well gas allocation, and rupee depreciation
EBITDA per scm margins saw a sharp deterioration but are now stabilising
Elevated Henry Hub prices in 4QFY26 remain a near-term hurdle; however, margins are expected to edge higher in FY27
We believe the market is underappreciating the possibility of modest price hikes
We model Mahanagar Gas Limited volumes to clock a 10% CAGR over FY26–28, with an estimated EBITDA margin of ₹8.5 per scm during the period
We also believe Indraprastha Gas Limited valuations are attractive at current levels

*Bernstein on Bajaj Finance*
Maintain Underperform; raised target price to ₹840 from ₹750 earlier
Near-term credit cost concerns have been alleviated, with accelerated provisions in 3Q26 addressing a key risk
BAF took a one-off ECL provision of 120bps (annualised), helping rebuild PCR after a period of decline
Asset quality concerns have eased in the near term, and a potential BHFL stake sale could provide an additional profitability buffer
However, concerns remain around sustaining 20%+ EPS growth, with NIMs facing secular decline pressure
Non-mortgage household credit penetration is already elevated, and growth in this segment is expected to remain moderate
Credit cost assumptions have been cut by 20bps for FY27–28E, while AUM growth has been raised by 2pp for FY27E
The revised target price is based on a 21x target P/E multiple versus 20x earlier

*Bernstein on PB Fintech*
Maintain target price at ₹2,210
Commission regulation risks now appear largely priced in, with downside seen only in the event of drastic take-rate cuts, which are considered unlikely
The stock is down 32% from its peak, and the correction is seen as factoring in most adverse scenarios
A 15% top-line hit is already reflected in valuations, while a 10–12% impact would imply a 20% reduction in term and health plan estimates
AI-based insurance wrappers are not viewed as a meaningful threat, with PB Fintech seen as best placed to build a similar platform
While AI-led changes to SEO remain a genuine concern due to lower organic traffic, improving conversion ratios could offset part of the impact
The company will need to lead industry-wide AI adoption to sustain its moat

Categorization and rationalization of mutual fund schemes

reports.channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

reports.text:
(2) introduction of ‘life cycle funds’ (open-ended schemes with attributes of pre-determined maturity and glide path strategy following goal-based investing with investments across various asset classes, viz. equity, debt, InvITs, ETCDs, gold and silver ETF),
(3) allowing MFs to offer both value and contra schemes provided scheme overlap is not >50% (currently only SBI AMC, Kotak AMC and Invesco India AMC offer contra schemes)
(4) limiting overlap of any sectoral/thematic equity scheme to 50% with any other existing sectoral/thematic or other equity schemes (excluding large cap); necessary to meet the criteria within 3 years 
Expect significant moderation in introduction of new sectoral schemes.

reports.text:
**Citi on India Brokers & Asset Managers: SEBI MF Reforms Impact**

SEBI's **MF categorisation overhaul** drives scheme rationalisation.

**Key Changes:**  
- **(1) Discontinue solution-oriented schemes** (₹0.6 tn AUM, 0.7% of Jan'26 total) — merge into similar schemes post approval  
- **(2) Introduce 'Life Cycle Funds'** — open-ended with maturity, glide path across equity/debt/InvITs/ETCDs/gold-silver ETF  
- **(3) Allow value & contra schemes** if overlap <50% (currently limited to SBI/Kotak/Invesco)  
- **(4) Cap sectoral/thematic overlap** at 50% with other equity (ex-large cap); comply within **3 years**

**Outlook:**  
Expect **fewer new sectoral schemes**.

**Key Takeaway:**  
Reforms promote differentiation and merger activity, curbing proliferation while adding goal-based Life Cycle option.

reports.text:
CITI on India Brokers & Asset Managers

Categorization and rationalization of mutual fund schemes

Key changes reflecting SEBI’s amendment includes
(1) discontinuation of solution-oriented schemes (industry MAAUM of Rs0.6 tn; 0.7% of overall Jan ’26 closing AUM) with all existing schemes to be merged with any existing scheme having similar asset allocation and risk profile, post SEBI approval,

(2) introduction of ‘life cycle funds’ (open-ended schemes with attributes of pre-determined maturity and glide path strategy following goal-based investing with investments across various asset classes, viz. equity, debt, InvITs, ETCDs, gold and silver ETF),

(3) allowing MFs to offer both value and contra schemes provided scheme overlap is not >50% (currently only SBI AMC, Kotak AMC and Invesco India AMC offer contra schemes)

(4) limiting overlap of any sectoral/thematic equity scheme to 50% with any other existing sectoral/thematic or other equity schemes (excluding large cap); necessary to meet the criteria within 3 years

Expect significant moderation in introduction of new sectoral schemes.

Gem Aromatics has been investing in its R&D and since last 2-2.5 years it has achieved major milestones by developing vapor-based technology to manufacture Anisole and find out other downstream products which has a meaningful potential for the future growth. For e.g. – BHA, synthetic Anithole, etc.
• It has not considered traditional products in the phenol chemistry value chain- such as Guaiacol, Catechol and Vanillin due to market challenges.

Stock is trading at 35.2x P/E of TTM EPS.

Gem Aromatics Ltd – Plant Visit Note
(Nirmal Bang Retail Research)

*# FY28E Guidance:  Rs. 1,100 cr of Revenue with margin in the range of 16% to 18%*
*#Capacity expansion to drive the growth: recent commissioning of the Dahej MPP plant (~10k MTPA) will drive the growth over the next three to four years. Prior to Dahej, capacity stands at ~5.3k MTPA.*

*Outlook: Positive over mid-term*

*Key Highlights*
• We visited a Multi-purpose Plant (MPP) plant of Gem Aromatics Ltd located at Dahej, Gujarat on 24th Feb’26, which commissioned its phase I production of clove and clove derivatives in Nov’25, followed by Phase II production of Cooling Agents, Phenol Derivatives and Citral Derivatives in Dec’25.
• Incorporated in 1997, Gem Aromatics Ltd is one of the players in specialty chemicals industry mainly focusing on flavour & fragrance (F&F) ingredients and aroma chemicals. 
• It is an established manufacturer of specialty ingredients, including, essential oils, aroma chemicals and value-added derivatives in India. Its portfolio of 70+ products spans across mint derivatives (incl. menthol), spice & citrus derivatives, and a growing set of aroma chemicals, supplied to oral care, pharma, cosmetics, nutraceuticals, wellness and pain management and personal care across India and more than 20 countries.
• The Company is managed by Mr. Yash Parekh - MD & CEO, Mrs. Kaksha Parekh - Chairperson and Chief Financial Officer and, Mr. Vipul Parekh – Whole Time Director. Mr. Vipul looks after capacity expansion plans, while Mr. Yash deals in sales and purchases.
• Domestic: Export mix stands at 50:50; where US has a major share in exports. It is witnessing demand recovery in exports.
• It has three manufacturing facilities, located at Silvassa (1500 MTPA), Budaun (3800 MTPA) and Dahej (10829 MTPA). Total capacity stands at ~16000 MTPA. 
• It’s newly commissioned plant at Dahej is a ~80% automated and implemented vapor-phase technology for the manufacturing of Anisole with a continuous flow chemistry. In continuous flow chemistry, shift towards production of new products will require 7 days.
• In the Citral Derivatives, it has built largest capacity in the world for Safranal to compete with global major like Givaudan for import substitution.
• In the plant area – it has an admin building, an area with 21 tanks processing various solvents as per requirement, a warehouse which can store ~3500 drums underground, a process plant area of 25K sq. ft. which has a 5-level building and boiler rooms. In the Warehouse, storage capacity stands at ~4-4.5K KGS, which includes raw material, work in progress, semi-finished goods and finished goods.
• It highlighted that Government has made a provision Common Effluent Treatment Plant (CETP) at Dahej location, which offers savings from developing a Zero Liquid Discharge (ZLD) plant at a cost of Rs. 15 per 1000 litre of water that goes into CETP. Also, safety is at the core with all necessary provisions in place.
• Clove and Clove Derivatives production is based on the batch process, which requires at least 2 days to shift the production of a new product.
• Product category wise FY28E revenue guidance for Dahej plant: Citral and Citral Derivatives - Rs. 50 cr, Phenol and Phenol Derivatives – Rs. 250 cr, Clove and Clove Derivatives – Rs. 150 cr and Cooling Agents – Rs. 175 cr and other specialty chemicals + CDMO + installed MPP – Rs. 175 cr.
• It has sourced all the required equipment locally and developed this MPP project at Dahej.
• So far, it has completed 8 technology transfers, which are at the initial stage and generated ~Rs. 3-3.5 cr of revenue from these green products. It is focusing on products which has a potential market size of the 50-1000 MTPA range.
• It has indicated that ~38 products are being ready and will be starting production in Q1FY27.

Knowledge Marine

Most powerful growth triggers from their latest Q3FY26 Concall:

1. “Green Tug” 15-Year Build-and-Charter Model (₹700 crore)

15-Year Annuity Revenue (Not Just Order Book)
Unlike dredging (project-based, cyclical),
these contracts are:
15-year tenure
Build + own + charter model
Predictable cash flows
High utilization (port operations = 365 days)

This transforms business mix from project contractor to asset-backed annuity operator
That structurally improves:
Earnings visibility
Valuation multiple
Cash flow stability

2. Shipyard capacity targeting ₹500-700 crore topline in 3 years

If executed:
Company scale could double
Vertical integration improves margins
Fleet cost reduces
But this is execution-dependent.

If:
Green tug portfolio crosses ₹1500-2000 crore
Shipyard capacity ramps successfully
Order book stays >₹1500 crore consistently
Then company shifts from: Small dredging contractor to Integrated marine infra plus annuity asset operator.

That is a business model re-rating trigger, not just earnings growth.

The most powerful statement in entire concall:
“Higher revenue and margin profile can be considered as benchmark going forward.”
If that holds true for 2-3 quarters,
market will stop valuing this as small-cap contractor and start valuing it as scalable marine infra platform.

reports.text:
SEBI introduces Life Cycle Funds. 

A new Life Cycle Fund category replaces existing Solution Oriented Funds (Retirement & Children’s Funds).

What are Life Cycle Funds?

Life Cycle Funds will:
•Have a defined target maturity ranging from 30 years to 5 years
•Follow a pre-determined glide path asset allocation 
•Automatically reduce equity exposure as maturity date approaches
•Increase allocation to debt and safer instruments over time
•Operate as open-ended schemes with a specified maturity

Target Maturities allowed (As per SEBI framework)

Life Cycle Funds can be structured across different target maturities such as:
•30 years
•25 years
•20 years
•15 years
•10 years
•5 years

This allows investors to choose a fund aligned to their retirement horizon.

What’s in it for investors:
•Removes static allocation problem of old retirement funds 
•Aligns risk to life stage
•Reduces emotional asset allocation decisions
•removes taxation issue in existing solution oriented funds when investors switch funds to change asset allocation

20 Key Changes in SEBI’s New MF Scheme Categorisation Circular

1.AMCs can now launch both Value and Contra Funds, with portfolio overlap capped at 50%. Earlier, only one was allowed.

2.New Sectoral Debt Funds introduced (Financial Services, Energy, Infrastructure, Housing, Real Estate) min 80% in AA+ & above.

3.New Life Cycle Funds category, six maturities (30, 25, 20, 15, 10, 5 years) with fixed glide path reducing equity over time.

4.Sectoral/Thematic funds can be launched only from the list notified by AMFI in consultation with SEBI (updated half-yearly)

5.Balanced Hybrid Funds allowed with 40-60% equity allocation. Earlier, AMCs had to choose between Balanced or Aggressive Hybrid.

6.Solution-Oriented Schemes (Retirement & Children’s Plans) discontinued, fresh subscriptions stopped and schemes to be merged with similar categories.

7.Mandatory monthly disclosure of category-wise portfolio overlap (equity vs equity, debt vs debt, hybrid vs hybrid) on AMC websites.

8.Equity schemes may deploy residual allocation in money market/liquid instruments, gold/silver instruments, and InvITs.

9.Sectoral/Thematic schemes must limit portfolio overlap to 50% with other equity schemes (except large-cap). Calculated quarterly as average of daily overlap.

10.Existing Sectoral/Thematic schemes get 3 years to comply; non-compliant schemes must merge.

11.Arbitrage Funds’ debt exposure restricted to government securities (<1-year maturity) and repo in government bonds only.

12.Low Duration Fund renamed to Ultra Short to Short Term Fund.

13.Long Duration Fund renamed to Long Term Fund.

14.Dynamic Bond Fund renamed to Dynamic Term Fund.

15.Balanced Advantage Fund renamed to Dynamic Asset Allocation Fund.

16.Credit Risk Funds must invest minimum 65% in AA and below-rated bonds (earlier: below AA+).

17.Floating Rate Funds may invest in fixed-rate instruments synthetically converted to floating exposure via swaps/derivatives.

18.Hybrid schemes may invest residual allocation in InvITs (except Arbitrage), ETCDs, Gold ETFs, and Silver ETFs.

19.Equity Savings Funds must maintain net equity exposure between 15-40%.

20.Foreign securities will not be treated as a separate asset class.

Chandan Healthcare Limited – BOD Approves Incorporation of Wholly Owned Subsidiary

Chandan Healthcare Limited’s Board of Directors has approved the incorporation of a Wholly Owned Subsidiary in the name of Chandan Healthcare Projects Private Limited.

The subsidiary will undertake development, operation and maintenance of Radiology Imaging Diagnostic Centres in selected hospitals across Punjab under PPP mode.

The Company will hold 100% shareholding upon incorporation with an initial investment of ₹1,00,000 comprising 10,000 equity shares of ₹10 each. Incorporation is subject to approval of the Registrar of Companies and upon completion, the entity will become a Wholly Owned Subsidiary of the Company.

The subsidiary will execute the recently secured PPP projects in Punjab, where the Company has won two consecutive contracts in different cities from Punjab Health Systems Corporation under the Department of Health & Family Welfare, Government of Punjab.

This strategic step strengthens the Company’s institutional diagnostics footprint under the PPP model and enhances long-term revenue visibility while creating a dedicated structure for project execution and scale-up in Punjab.

Nandani Creation’s Jaipur Kurti Brand Enters a New Growth Phase

Nandani Creation Ltd, the Jaipur-based women’s ethnic wear company operating under the flagship Jaipur Kurti brand, has achieved a key milestone with the brand crossing ₹100 crore sales in Calendar Year 2025, as per recent company disclosures.

Key Highlights:
* NSE-listed women’s apparel company headquartered in Jaipur
* Focused on ethnic and fusion wear including kurtis, palazzos, tops and occasion wear
* Operates a multi-brand portfolio including:
– Jaipur Kurti (Core Brand)
– Jaipur Kurti Luxe
– Amaiva by Jaipur Kurti
– Desi Fusion

Distribution Network:
* Strong omnichannel presence across online marketplaces and own website
* 180+ shop-in-shop counters / large format retail presence
* Expanding Exclusive Brand Outlet (EBO) footprint across key Indian markets
* Retail partnerships include leading chains such as Shoppers Stop, Reliance Trends, Centro, Avantara and Kalanikethan

Business Model:
* Focus on design-driven product offerings
* Mix of owned retail, large format retail partnerships and digital sales channels
* Strategic sourcing model supporting operational flexibility

The ₹100+ crore milestone reflects continued brand scale-up and strengthening retail presence in India’s growing women’s ethnic wear segment.

Regulatory Probes (Negative)  
- CBI registers case vs Anil Ambani post-ED action on Rs 2,000 Cr loan default – adds pressure on Reliance group entities.

- Dealers flag 2-3% YoY drop in Feb 2026 volumes despite low base; signals demand softness amid power sector moderation.

- GMR Airports: Dealers anticipate imminent block deal – potential liquidity event for infra aviation play.

Banking Flows (Positive)  
- RBL Bank sees strong buy interest via fil desk; large foreign investor accumulation in trade window boosts sentiment.

- NSE CEO Chauhan: Expect further F&O degrowth post-Apr tax hike; speculation curbs to persist – BSE dips on cue, broking under lens.

- Strong turnaround with profitability rebound, healthy cash flows, steady orders.  
- Revamps/Spares/Services drives growth; steel recovery aids outlook.

Market Close

HPV Vaccine Tailwind (Sector Positive)  
- Health Secy pushes states for free HPV campaign for 14-yr girls; PM launch Feb 28 from Ajmer – pharma/vaccine plays in focus.

Flat close masks F&O headwinds and volume concerns; infra/banking flows offer selective opportunities amid probe risks.

YES Bank Clarifies Forex Card Fraud News; Says No Material Impact

• 🔍 Unauthorized Transactions Detected: Bank flagged suspicious activity on multi-currency prepaid forex cards issued with BookMyForex.

• 🌎 Incident Details: Fraud attempts originated from 15 merchants in a Latin American country during early hours of Feb 24.

• 🛡️ Controls Limited Damage: ~USD 0.28 mn (₹2.5 Cr) approved across ~5,000 customers; 688 attempts declined, preventing ~USD 0.1 mn loss.

• 🚫 Preventive Action Taken: Bank restricted e-commerce transactions from the specific geography and initiated chargeback process.

• 📢 No Additional Undisclosed Info: Bank stated no unreported events affecting share price.

🟡 Impact: Neutral
Incident is small and contained; strong monitoring and limited financial exposure reduce material risk, though reputational watch remains.

Premier Energies Unveils India’s First Zero Busbar Solar Cell

• 🔬 Technology Breakthrough: Company launches India’s first Zero Busbar (0BB) solar cell at Intersolar Gandhinagar.

• ⚡ Higher Efficiency: New architecture reduces shading losses and delivers higher power output vs conventional cells.

• 💰 Lower Silver Usage: 0BB design significantly cuts silver consumption, improving cost economics.

• 🛠️ Better Reliability: Enhances mechanical flexibility, lowers interconnection stress, and improves resistance to micro-cracks.

• 🏭 Manufacturing Edge: Demonstrates strong process innovation and strengthens leadership in next-gen solar tech.

• 📈 Strategic Fit: Supports company’s broader ₹12,500 crore capex plan to scale solar manufacturing ecosystem.

Bangladesh May Renegotiate Adani Power Deal

Bangladesh’s new government plans to review its power purchase agreement with Adani Power to lower electricity costs.

Under a 25-year deal signed during Sheikh Hasina’s tenure, Bangladesh imports the entire 1,600 MW output from Adani’s Jharkhand coal plant. Supply began in 2023 but was briefly disrupted in 2024 over unpaid dues before resuming.

The administration led by Tarique Rahman wants to renegotiate terms seen as expensive, though this could strain ties with India.

Adani Power says it has received no formal notice and continues to meet contractual obligations.

Voltas Implemented Price Hikes

Pricing & Margins
Implemented price hikes due to:
Higher commodity costs
Rupee depreciation
Changes in energy-efficiency norms
Some impact offset by GST rate cuts.

Voltas Limited Targets 25% AC Sales Growth on Early Summer Demand

Growth Outlook

Voltas expects 25% growth in room air-conditioner (RAC) sales this summer.

The outlook is driven by the early onset of summer and rising temperatures, particularly in North India.

Product Strategy

Launched a new AI-powered AC range capable of operating efficiently at temperatures up to 55°C.

Focused on strengthening its full-stack consumer portfolio, according to MD Mukundan Menon.

Market Dynamics

Tier-II and Tier-III cities are major demand drivers.

Nearly 85% of first-time AC buyers come from these markets, indicating strong penetration-led growth potential.

Sales & Capacity

Sold 2.6 million AC units in the previous financial year.

Targeting sales closer to 3 million units with expected demand surge.

New Chennai plant adds 100,000 units of capacity, with scalability up to 2 million units annually if required.

- 𝗦𝘁𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗲𝗴𝗶𝗰 𝗣𝗶𝘃𝗼𝘁: RGL is leveraging global partnerships & a scaling digital ecosystem to capture the highest value segments of the global jewellery market.

- 𝗙𝗶𝗻𝗮𝗻𝗰𝗶𝗮𝗹 𝗣𝗿𝗼𝗷𝗲𝗰𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝘀:
▸ 𝗙𝗬29 𝗥𝗲𝘃𝗲𝗻𝘂𝗲 𝗧𝗮𝗿𝗴𝗲𝘁𝘀: ₹1,600 Cr from Owned & Licensed Brands & ₹1,000 Cr from Customer Brands.

▸ 𝗘𝗕𝗜𝗧𝗗𝗔 𝗠𝗮𝗿𝗴𝗶𝗻𝘀: Targeting 15-18% for D2C operations vs. 7-9% for B2B.

▸ 𝗚𝗿𝗼𝘀𝘀 𝗠𝗮𝗿𝗴𝗶𝗻𝘀: Targeting 60-65% for D2C vs. 20-25% for B2B.

Chairman & MD Arvind Poddar said the strategy draws from BKT’s earlier India playbook — focusing on differentiated products and adopting a distributor-led model.

The move reflects diversification amid shifting global trade patterns and uneven overseas demand.

About one year into the capex cycle, the remaining investment will be deployed over the next 15–18 months.

Balkrishna Industries Enters Consumer Tyre Segment, Targets ₹23,000 Cr Revenue by FY30

Balkrishna Industries (BKT) is entering the consumer tyre market, expanding beyond its core off-highway tyres (OHT) business.

The company aims to grow revenues to ₹23,000 crore by FY30 through scale expansion and brand investment.

BKT has earmarked ₹3,500 crore capex over three years for new product lines, capacity expansion, and brand-building initiatives.

The consumer foray will cover two-wheelers and medium & heavy commercial vehicles (MHCVs) — segments marked by high volumes and intense competition.

The transition includes a national branding campaign featuring actor Ranveer Singh, signalling a strong push into the domestic B2C space.

Global Supply Chain Diversification
• Diversified supply chain across Middle East, Africa, Europe.
• Reduces single geography dependence.
• Exports remained resilient despite high tariffs.

Strategic Expansion

Guidance & Capex
• No change in capex plans.

• Confident of outperforming stated guidance.

Regulatory Engagement
• Ongoing discussions with Indian and US authorities.
• Proactive approach toward policy developments.

🌟 What Do We Do With the Subscription and Donation Money? 🌟

You might be wondering — where does the subscription or donation money go? Here’s how we use it:

Every 2 months, we organize a donation drive where we:

🐶 Feed and care for stray dogs with food, medicine, and support
📚 Distribute stationery and essential items to students in need

All the subscription money, along with donations, is used for these drives. What may seem like a small contribution to us can mean the world to them. Your support can bring warmth, food, and comfort to a life that needs it.

Let’s join hands and make their days a little brighter — together! 💙🌸🐾

Basmati Exporters Demand Dedicated Board

Industry Scale  
₹60,000 Cr exports (~6 Mn tonnes annually); supports 1.5-2 Mn farmers in GI regions (Haryana, Punjab, W-UP, J&K).

Under APEDA's broad mandate – lacks GI focus, agronomy, enforcement for seeds/residues/adulteration.  

Board Proposal  
- Like Tea/Coffee Boards: Seed protocols, DNA database, buffer zones, phytosanitary compliance.  
- Basmati Research Institute w/ ICAR for breeding/pest/residues.  

Rationale  
Rising Pakistan rivalry; GI threats (EU/Aus/NZ delays); need farmer training/infra/research.  

Key Takeaway  
Dedicated board shields GI/market share in premium export – govt push could boost quality/₹ realization for 1.5Mn farmers.

Government Halves RoDTEP Benefits, Exporters Brace for Impact

The government has cut benefits under the Remission of Duties and Taxes on Exported Products (RoDTEP) scheme by 50% with immediate effect.

Applicable RoDTEP rates will now be capped at half of the existing rates, including notified value caps.

Earlier, refunds ranged from 0.3% to 4.3% of the value of exported products; these will now fall to 0.15% to 2.15%.

RoDTEP reimburses taxes, duties and levies incurred at central, state and local levels that are not refunded under other mechanisms.

Refunds are issued as transferable duty credit scrips, which exporters can use to pay import duties or sell in the market.

The move has raised concerns among exporters already navigating a challenging global trade environment.

France May Lose Tax Edge as India Scraps MFN Clause in DTAA

India plans to remove the Most Favoured Nation (MFN) clause from its Double Taxation Avoidance Convention (DTAC) with France, ending capital gains tax benefits for France-based FPIs.

If implemented, French portfolio investors in Indian markets will have to pay capital gains tax in India.

France-based FPIs held about $21 billion in Indian equities as of January 2026.

The move could generate significant additional tax revenue for India.

Several FPIs had routed investments via France after India tightened tax treaties with Mauritius and Singapore in 2017.

Some investments are also routed through Participatory Notes (P-notes) issued by Sebi-registered FPIs.

Under the current treaty, FPIs from France with up to a 10% stake in Indian firms pay no capital gains tax due to earlier bilateral provisions.

The proposed change signals India’s broader effort to plug tax arbitrage routes and align foreign investment taxation across jurisdictions.

Tata Motors, M&M Indonesia Deal Stalls Amid Import Backlash

A proposed ₹25 trillion Indonesian Rupiah (~$1.5 billion) truck procurement deal involving Tata Motors and Mahindra & Mahindra has faltered due to domestic opposition in Indonesia.

The contract, one of the largest global supply deals, involved orders for 35,000 four-wheel-drive pickups and 35,000 six-wheel trucks from each company.

State-owned Agrinas Pangan Nusantara had placed orders as part of Indonesia’s food security and rural logistics programme.

Indonesia’s automotive lobby strongly opposed large-scale imports of completely built units (CBUs), arguing it would hurt local manufacturers already facing slowing demand.

A local think tank (Celios) warned the move could shrink Indonesia’s GDP by $2.3 billion and displace up to 330,000 workers.

Indonesia’s automotive sales are expected to decline about 6.8% in 2025, intensifying concerns over competition from imports, especially cheaper unofficial Chinese imports.

Industry bodies argue local firms can meet demand if given time, pushing the government to reconsider the import-heavy proposal.

- Coforge: ₹2,278 (from ₹2,426) – Outperform (High Conviction).  
- HCL Tech: ₹1,506 (from ₹1,661) – Hold.  
- Infosys: ₹1,653 (from ₹1,779) – Outperform.  
- LTIMindtree: ₹6,304 (from ₹7,067) – Outperform.  
- Persistent: ₹8,058 (from ₹8,865) – Outperform (High Conviction).  
- TCS: ₹3,333 (from ₹3,593) – Outperform.  
- Tech Mahindra: ₹1,698 (from ₹1,810) – Outperform.  
- Wipro: ₹218 (from ₹231) – Hold.  

**Impact**  
- Cautiously Negative (Near Term) – Broad TP cuts signal valuation pressure despite steady AI bets.  

**Key Takeaway**  
CLSA trims IT TPs amid downside risks but sticks to AI leaders like TechM, Infosys, Persistent for upcycle play.

If those weaken while AI capability keeps accelerating, the displacement scenario becomes real.

The risk is not AI failing.
The risk is AI working faster than institutions can adjust.

🚨 AI IS ABOUT TO TRIGGER THE BIGGEST ECONOMIC CRISIS SINCE 2008.

Not because AI is failing. But because AI is moving at a pace the traditional economy is not adjusting to fast enough.

This is what Citrini layed out in its research and here's everything you need to know about it.

White collar workers make up roughly half of US employment. But more importantly, the top 20% of income earners drive around 60%+ of total consumer spending in the US.

That means a relatively small group supports a very large part of:
• housing demand
• car purchases
• travel
• restaurants
• software subscriptions
• private school
• mortgage quality

Now AI coding tools are already reducing the cost of building software. Companies are renegotiating large SaaS contracts because internal teams using AI can now replicate core features faster and cheaper.

When companies cut 10-15% of staff, seat based software revenue drops automatically.

EARLY IMPACT: Margins improve because payroll drops.

SECOND IMPACT: High-income wages weaken.

THIRD IMPACT: Spending slows.

But machines produce output. Machines do not consume output. If a $180,000 product manager role is replaced by a $200/month AI tool, corporate profit rises but household income falls.

If this happens at scale, you get a loop:

AI improves → companies cut jobs → spending weakens → companies protect margins → buy more AI → cut more jobs.

Each step makes sense for one company. But across the whole economy, it shrinks the wage base that supports demand.

Now look at what just happened. After Claude announced its new Code Security AI update, cybersecurity stocks sold off sharply.

Why? Because investors immediately realized AI is now automating tasks inside cybersecurity itself, a sector that was considered protected from automation risk.

That is the displacement effect spreading beyond software into security, compliance, and enterprise defense roles. This is how the loop expands.

Now add credit.

US private credit has grown to over $2 trillion. Many deals in software and tech were priced assuming stable recurring revenue. If AI compresses pricing power, those revenue assumptions weaken.

If defaults rise, private credit funds, insurers, and pension holders face pressure.

Now look at housing. The US mortgage market is around $13 trillion. Mortgages are underwritten on stable income assumptions.

If high income job stability weakens, lenders tighten standards. If housing demand weakens while incomes are under pressure, prices soften.

That affects household wealth and confidence.

Now look at government revenue. The US government collects most of its money from payroll taxes and income taxes. If labor income shrinks while capital income rises, tax collection changes.

At the same time, displaced workers need support. That means higher spending and lower tax receipts at the same time.

This is different from 2008.
2008 was a financial system failure.
This would be a labor structure shift.
In past tech revolutions, new jobs replaced old jobs quickly.

The risk here is speed.

If AI replaces high-income cognitive roles faster than new high-income roles are created, spending drops before the system adapts.

This does not guarantee collapse. But it increases the risk of:

• weaker discretionary spending
• pricing pressure in software and services
• rising stress in private credit
• housing sensitivity in high-income cities
• fiscal deficits widening during a productivity boom

AI can be extremely productive and still create short-term economic instability. The system was built on scarce human intelligence.

If intelligence becomes cheap and widely available through machines, the value of human labor changes. Markets will reprice that shift.

If you want to track whether this risk is building, watch simple data:

• white collar job openings
• wage growth in high-income sectors
• enterprise software renewal pricing
• private credit default rates
• housing delinquencies in tech-heavy metros
• payroll tax receipts

The Global AI Crisis - Citrini Research

H1 FY26 revenue grew 72% YoY to ₹5,410L, while PAT rose 116% to ₹1,302L. EBITDA margin expanded to 35.5% and PAT margin stood at 24%, reflecting solid operating leverage.

FY25 revenue was ₹7,699L with PAT of ₹1,642L, indicating a sharp improvement over previous years.

Capacity expansion is underway with existing 562 mn units and additional 1,360 mn units approved under ₹75 Cr capex. Trial production has already begun.

Global certifications like MDSAP and SABS strengthen export potential, with UNFPA qualification in advanced stages.

**Motilal Oswal BUY Lenskart**
**BUY, TP ₹600 (27% upside from ₹474) **

**Core Investment Case**  
- India's largest organized eyewear retailer, fully integrated from design/manufacturing to omnichannel (app + 3,100+ stores globally).  
- Underpenetrated: 53% Indians need vision correction but only 35% use glasses; organized share 24%.  
- Tech-led model with centralized mfg, backward integration, house-of-brands for cost/experience moats.  
- 25% consol revenue CAGR, 53% pre-IND AS EBITDA CAGR FY25-28; 625 bps margin expansion.  
- BUY, TP ₹600 (27% upside from ₹474) on DCF 55x FY28E pre-IND AS EBITDA.  

**Market TAM & Frictions**  
- Global refractive errors to 55% (~4.7 bn) by FY30; India/SE Asia 30% incidence, 35-40% penetration vs 70-80% developed.  
- TAM ₹2.3 tn+; Lenskart 5% India share, <2% international.  
- Frictions: low awareness/stigma, optometrist shortage (30-50/mn vs 100+), low store density, high ASPs, import-heavy 3-4x frame markups.  

**Business Moats**  
- Centralized hubs handle 75% JIT orders; FY25 in-house 6.4 mn frames/4.1 mn lenses cuts costs 35-40%.  

**More Insights ****⤵️****
****@Concalls3**
- Hyderabad facility adds 50 mn pairs export capacity.  
- Remote optometry in 168 stores; eye-tests 13.3 mn FY25 (63% CAGR), 47% first-time 9MFY26.  
- 3,144 stores; app 100 mn+ downloads.  

**Store Economics**  
- India store: ₹39.4 mn revenue, 33% pre-IND AS EBITDA margin, 10-month payback.  
- >4,200 incremental stores potential; 1,480+ net India adds FY25-28.  

**Brands & Tech**  
- 24 brands: John Jacobs (premium), Vincent Chase (value), Phonic (smart glasses UPI), Hustlr (Gen-Z).  
- AI try-ons, recommendations; newer cohorts buy 3.6 units/2 yrs vs industry 1.8.  

**Forecasts**  
- India revenue 27% CAGR; int'l 22% CAGR FY25-28.  
- Consol revenue ₹133 bn FY28; pre-IND AS EBITDA ₹18.4 bn (13.8% margin); PAT ₹10.4 bn.  
- FCF improves post-FY28 capex normalization.  

**Valuation & Risks**  
- FY28E EV/pre-IND AS EBITDA 42x; premium justified vs peers on growth.  
- Risks: China raw materials, North India mfg concentration, overseas losses, optometrist shortage.  

**Key Takeaway**  
Lenskart's TAM + integration moats + 10-month payback drive multi-year FCF ramp - BUY ₹600 justified.

****@Stockupdate9**

**Exposure, deposits and quantification
**• Haryana-related deposits constitute ~0.5% of total bank deposits.
• The observed outflow from these accounts is approximately INR 2bn.
• The total exposure is estimated at ~INR 5.9bn, comprising:
o INR 4.9bn identified initially, and
o An additional ~INR 1bn identified subsequently.
• Management has clearly communicated the outer limit of the incident without delay.
• Government deposits (state + central) typically account for 8–10% of overall deposits.
 
**Detection, background and timeline**
• The bank was first notified by the Haryana State Government, with the issue gaining momentum post 18 February.
• One customer highlighted discrepancies in account statements, after which the bank reconciled other related accounts.
• There was no error in system-generated statements; alerts, SMS notifications and transaction statements were duly sent.
• No communication has been received from any other state government regarding similar issues.
 
**Nature of the incident
**• The incident is limited to a single branch and involved multiple accounts.
• This was not a system failure but a case of employee collusion with third parties.
• The transactions were executed through the cheque-based system, not electronic channels.
• The bank has stated this is a one-off incident, given its otherwise strong operating track record.
• The incident has deeply disturbed management, and decisive steps will be taken to address collusion-related risks.
 
**Recovery actions and Inter-bank coordination**
• The bank is analyzing the incident across Level 1, 2 and 3 scenarios to identify funds not legitimately disbursed.
• Recovery efforts are underway, including from other linked accounts.
• Funds were transferred to multiple other banks, all of other banks which have been cooperative.
• Certain recipient accounts required additional confirmations as part of the recovery process.
• The bank expects further recoveries from the identified accounts.
 
**Controls, checks and preventive framework
**• Transactions above INR 0.5mn are covered under a Positive Pay system, with customer cross-verification.
• Transactions above INR 1mn undergo additional verification layers.
• The bank has introduced a monthly balance monitoring system as part of fraud prevention.
• Going forward, the bank will introduce digitally verified stipulations for transaction authorisation.
• Enhanced measures are being implemented specifically to prevent collusion risks.
• Government accounts continue to receive heightened sensitivity and oversight.
** 
Forensic audit and accountability**
• A forensic audit has been initiated, covering the entire chain of events.
• KPMG has been appointed, with the process expected to take 4–5 weeks.
• The forensic review will verify facts, determine responsibility and assess intent, with a focus on fairness.
• The bank is engaging with employees and counterparties involved and will proceed strictly as per the law.
 
**Financial Impact, Insurance and capital
**• Bank holds an employee dishonesty insurance policy of approximately INR 350mn.
• The bank’s LCR stands at ~115%, well above regulatory requirements.
• Capital adequacy remains comfortable and in excess of minimum norms.
• Core operations remain strong, though the bank may take appropriate provisions.
 
**Business outlook and management confidence
**• Bank maintains a strong reputation in the market, despite recent interest rate reductions.
• Prior to the incident, the bank was expecting a strong quarter, supported by better NIMs and lower credit costs.
• Management reiterated its confidence in the strength of core operations and long-term franchise stability.

**IDFC FIRST BANK CONCALL ON FRAUD OF INR5.9B
** 
**CONCALL KTA
Remarks by management
**• The matter is restricted to the Chandigarh branch, with no involvement of any other branches.
• Employees from this branch were involved and have since been suspended.
• The bank has appointed KPMG to conduct an audit.
• Police complaints have already been filed in connection with the incident.
• Recovery efforts have commenced, and existing control mechanisms remain in place.
• The identified discrepancy stands at INR 4.9bn, with the bank conservatively recognising an additional INR 1bn, taking the total estimated impact to INR 5.9bn. This figure is considered broadly accurate at present.
• The bank remains well-capitalized, with profitability continuing on a positive trajectory.
• This was an isolated branch-level group incident, where debit instructions originated from the client and funds were transferred to external parties, with involvement of branch employees.
• The incident occurred with the connivance of certain employees, and the bank has moved swiftly by initiating a forensic audit.
• Necessary corrective actions will be taken to contain the issue and prevent any recurrence.
• The Board has taken cognisance of the matter and is acting in line with regulatory guidance.
• The bank operates over 1,000 branches and has not encountered a similar fraud in the past.
• The bank expects NIMs of approximately 5.8% for the current quarter.
